transforms.code:
def transform(self, value, quality, timestamp):
	        # Import necessary classes for date manipulation
	        from java.time import LocalDate
	        from java.time.temporal import ChronoUnit
	        from java.util import Date
	        from java.text import SimpleDateFormat
	    
	        # Attempt to get the date picker components safely
	        startDatePicker = self.getSibling("StartDatePicker") if self.getSibling("StartDatePicker") else None
	        endDatePicker = self.getSibling("EndDatePicker") if self.getSibling("EndDatePicker") else None
	    
	        # Check if both date picker components are found
	        if startDatePicker and endDatePicker:
	            startDate = startDatePicker.props.value
	            endDate = endDatePicker.props.value
	    
	            # Ensure both dates are selected before performing calculations
	            if startDate and endDate:
	                try:
	                    # Convert date strings to LocalDate
	                    dateFormatter = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSXXX")
	                    
	                    startDate = LocalDate.parse(dateFormatter.format(Date(startDate)))
	                    endDate = LocalDate.parse(dateFormatter.format(Date(endDate)))
	    
	                    # Calculate the duration between the start and end dates in days
	                    duration = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(startDate, endDate)
	    
	                    # Debug log to check the calculated duration
	                    print("Duration in days:", duration)
	    
	                    # Check if the duration exceeds 60 days
	                    if duration > 60:
	                        # Close the dock
	                        system.perspective.closeDock('rightDock')  # Use your dock's ID here
	    
	                        # Clear the selected dates from the input components
	                        startDatePicker.props.value = None
	                        endDatePicker.props.value = None
	    
	                        # Open a popup to notify the user about the error
	                        system.perspective.openPopup(
	                            id='dateErrorPopup',
	                            view='Muskan/DateRangeErrorPopup',  # Replace with the path to your popup view
	                            params={"message": "The selected date range exceeds 60 days. Please choose a shorter range."},
	                            title='Date Range Error',
	                            modal=True
	                        )
	                except Exception as e:
	                    # Handle parsing errors
	                    system.perspective.openPopup(
	                        id='dateParsingErrorPopup',
	                        view='Muskan/DateRangeErrorPopup',  # Replace with the path to your popup view
	                        params={"message": "Error parsing dates: {str(e)}"},
	                        title='Parsing Error',
	                        modal=True
	                    )
	        else:
	            # Open a popup if date pickers are not found
	            system.perspective.openPopup(
	                id='datePickerErrorPopup',
	                view='Muskan/DateRangeErrorPopup',  # Replace with the path to your popup view
	                params={"message": "Date pickers not found!"},
	                title='Component Error',
	                modal=True
	            )

config.script:
from java.util import Date
	    from java.text import SimpleDateFormat
	    Area = self.parent.parent.getChild("selectionCnt").getChild("AreaDD").props.value
	    Station = self.parent.parent.getChild("selectionCnt").getChild("stationDD").props.value
	    Line = self.parent.parent.getChild("selectionCnt").getChild("lineDD").props.value
	    StartDate = self.getSibling("StartDatePicker").props.value
	    EndDate = self.getSibling("EndDatePicker").props.value
	    system.perspective.print("startDate= %s"%StartDate)
	    system.perspective.print("EndDate= %s"%EndDate)
	    SerialNum=self.parent.parent.getChild("selectionCnt").getChild("SerialTextField").props.text
	    if not Line:
	       Line = 'All'
	    tableComp = self.parent.parent.getChild("TableCnt").getChild("Table")
	    msgStatus = self.parent.parent.getChild("msgStatus")
	    if StartDate and EndDate:           
	      date_format = SimpleDateFormat('yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')
	      StartDate = date_format.format(StartDate)
	      EndDate = date_format.format(EndDate) 
	      system.perspective.print("FormatstartDate= %s"%StartDate)
	      system.perspective.print("FormatEndDate= %s"%EndDate)
	    else:
			msgStatus.props.text = 'Please select Start Date and End Date'
			setMessage("Error", msgStatus.props.text)
			return
	    	  	    
	    if Area != '':
                 if SerialNum != '':
	                           # Query using Area and Serial Number
                 	       data = system.db.runNamedQuery("Project_VECV/Report/LogNCNew/query_GetDataBySerialNumAndArea_LogNC", {"selectedArea": Area, "selectedSerialNum": SerialNum,"Startdate":StartDate,"ToDate":EndDate})
                 	                          
                 	       if data and len(data) > 0:
                 	           tableComp.props.data = data
                 	           msgStatus.props.text = 'Data filtered successfully for Area and Serial Number.'
                 	           setMessage("Success", msgStatus.props.text)
                 	       else:
                 	           msgStatus.props.text = 'No data found for the specified Area and Serial Number.'
                 	           setMessage("Error", msgStatus.props.text)                 
                 else:
	                           # Handle Line and Station conditions when Serial Number is not provided
	                           if Line != 'All' and Station != '':
	                               # Query using Area, Line, and Station
	                               data = system.db.runNamedQuery("Project_VECV/Report/LogNCNew/query_GetDataByAreaLineAndStation_LogNC", {"selectedArea": Area, "selectedLine": Line, "selectedStation": Station,"Startdate":StartDate,"ToDate":EndDate})
	                               errorMsg = 'No data found for the specified Area, Line, and Station.'
	                           elif Line != 'All':
	                               # Query using Area and Line only
	                               data = system.db.runNamedQuery("Project_VECV/Report/LogNCNew/query_GetDataByAreaAndLine_LogNC", {"selectedArea": Area, "selectedLine": Line,"Startdate":StartDate,"ToDate":EndDate})
	                               errorMsg = 'No data found for the specified Area and Line.'
	                           elif Station != '':
	                               # Query using Area and Station only
	                               data = system.db.runNamedQuery("Project_VECV/Report/LogNCNew/query_GetDataByAreaAndStation_LogNC", {"selectedArea": Area, "selectedStation": Station,"Startdate":StartDate,"ToDate":EndDate})
	                               errorMsg = 'No data found for the specified Area and Station.'
	                           else:
	                               # If neither Line nor Station is provided, display an error message
	                               data = None
	                               errorMsg = 'Please specify a valid Line or Station for the selected Area.'
	               
	                           # Check and display the appropriate message based on the query results
	                           if data and len(data) > 0:
	                               tableComp.props.data = data
	                               msgStatus.props.text = 'Data filtered successfully.'
	                               setMessage("Success", msgStatus.props.text)
	                           else:
	                               msgStatus.props.text = errorMsg
	                               setMessage("Error", msgStatus.props.text)	      
	    else:
                 msgStatus.props.text = 'Area should not be null.'
                 setMessage("Error", msgStatus.props.text)
             
def setMessage(status, message):
                 system.perspective.sendMessage(
                     messageType="setMessage",
                     payload={
                         "message": message,
                         "status": status
                     },
                     scope="view"
                 )

import system
      
      
      # Get the table data from the component
     
      tableData = self.parent.parent.getChild("TableCnt").getChild("Table").props.data
      
      if tableData:
          # Extract headers dynamically from table columns
          headers = list(tableData.getColumnNames()) if tableData.getRowCount() > 0 else []
          
          # Prepare rows of data from the table
          rows = []
          for row in tableData:
              rows.append([row[col] for col in headers])
          
          # Create a dataset from headers and rows
          dataset = system.dataset.toDataSet(headers, rows)
          
          # Convert the dataset to CSV format
          csvData = system.dataset.toCSV(dataset, showHeaders=True)
          
          # Specify the file path and write CSV data to a file
          filePath = system.file.getTempFile("LogNCReport.csv")
          system.file.writeFile(filePath, csvData)
          
          # Read the file as bytes for download
          fileName = "LogNCReport.csv"
          csvBytes = system.file.readFileAsBytes(filePath)
          
          # Trigger the download in the Perspective view
          system.perspective.download(fileName, csvBytes)
          
          message = 'File downloaded successfully'
          setMessage("Success",message)
      else:
          message = 'Please first retrieve data and then download the file'
          setMessage("Error",message)
          
def setMessage(status,message):
        	system.perspective.sendMessage(
        	    messageType="setMessage",
           		payload={
                	"message": message,
                	"status": status
            	},
            	scope="view"  
        	)

messageHandlers.script:
message = payload['message']  
		status = payload['status']
			
		# Update the Text Field's text
		self.props.text = message
		self.meta.visible=True
			# Apply the style class (from the payload or a default value)
		self.props.style.classes = "Project_VECV/Reports/successMsg" if status=='Success' else "Project_VECV/Reports/errMsg"
